message.description: Deprecated; prefer using the errors array instead

url.description: Proof will make a POST request to this URL for every event.

header.description: Proof will add this header to every POST request.

webhook_v2_subscriptions.description:
An array containing the desired events that Proof should POST a webhook for.
See our [Webhooks V2 guide](https://dev.proof.com/docs/webhooks-v2) for a list of valid subscriptions.

webhook_v2_params.description: An object containing Webhook V2 creation parameters.

url.description: Proof will make a POST request to this URL for every subscribed event.

header.description:
A header value to pass through every request Proof makes back to your webhook URL.
This can be required authentication to your system for security purposes. (E.g. "header": "X-Custom-Header:X-Custom-Key")

request_id_header_name.description: The name of the header that will contain the request ID for each webhook request. Defaults to X-Request-Id.

headers.description:
A list of header values to pass through every request Proof makes back to your webhook URL.
This can be required authentication to your system for security purposes. (E.g. "headers": ["[X-Custom-Header]:[X-Custom-Key]"]). If passed, value for header (singular) is ignored.

signing_key.description:
A signing key to sign the webhook payloads with. This is used to verify the authenticity of the webhook requests.
If not passed and if using api keys to authenticate, Proof will use the api key to sign the payloads. https://dev.proof.com/docs/webhooks-v2#security

oauth_config.description: OAuth 2.0 client-credentials block. Supply either this object or an API key/header to authenticate incoming webhook requests.

oauth_token_url.description: Token endpoint URL where Proof will request bearer tokens.

webhook_v2_object.description: An object representing a Webhook V2.

id.description: The ID of the Webhook V2 object.

url.description: Proof will make a POST request to this URL for every subscribed event.

header.description:
A header value to pass through every request Proof makes back to your webhook URL.
This can be required authentication to your system for security purposes. (E.g. "header": "X-Custom-Header:X-Custom-Key")

headers.description:
A list of header values to pass through every request Proof makes back to your webhook URL.
This can be required authentication to your system for security purposes. (E.g. "headers": ["[X-Custom-Header]:[X-Custom-Key]"])

enabled.description: A boolean value to indicate if the webhook is active.

request_id_header_name.description: The name of the header that will contain the request ID for each webhook request. Defaults to X-Request-Id.

webhook_v2_event.description: An object representing a Webhook V2 event.

event.description: The Webhook V2 event type. See our [Webhooks V2 guide](https://dev.proof.com/docs/webhooks-v2) for a list of valid events.

body.description: The body of the response from your webhook receiver.

code.description: The status code of the response from your webhook receiver.

delivered.description: The date and time of the event delivery in ISO-8601 format.

webhook_v2_signing_status_event.description: An object representing a Webhook V2 event that includes signing status for signers.

body.description: The body of the response from your webhook receiver.

code.description: The status code of the response from your webhook receiver.

delivered.description: The date and time of the event delivery in ISO-8601 format.

payload.description: The payload of the event. Contains structured data with signing status information.

transaction_id.description: The ID of the transaction associated with the event.

transaction_detailed_status.description: The detailed status of the transaction.

date_occurred.description: The date and time when the event occurred.

external_id.description: A string representing an ID external to Proof's systems.

signing_status.description: The current signing status for the signer.

date_occurred.description: The date and time of the event in ISO-8601 format.

organization_name.description: Name of customer organization account on Proof platform

signers.description: A list of signers associated with an event

external_id.description: External signer id supplied by Proof customer

kba_passed.description: A boolean value to indicate if signer passed KBA

full_verification_process_passed.description: A boolean value to indicate if signer passed the verification process fully

credential_analysis_passed.description: A boolean value to indicate if signer passed Credential Analysis

selfie_passed.description: A boolean value to indicate if signer passed selfie check

suppress_email.description: If sent as true, Proof will not send a notification email to the signer when the transaction is activated. If you use this feature, you are responsible for providing the signer with the `transaction_access_link`. This value is not stored on the transaction.

require_new_signer_verification.description: Does the signer need to verify email ownership

skip_closing_ops.description: Send to closing ops or not. Defaults to false.

id.description: The ID of the recording location.

name.description: The name of the recording location.

state.description: The US state of the recording location.

supported.description: Whether erecording is supported at the recording location

recording_type.description: The type of recording supported at the location. ELECTRONIC, PAPER, or NONE.

eligible.description: Allowed legally and by organizations preferences.

eligible_title_agencies.description: The title agencies that are eligible for the recording location.

eligible_title_underwriters.description: The title underwriters that are eligible for the recording location.

id.description: The ID of the title agency.

name.description: The name of the title agency.

alta_id.description: The ALTA ID of the title agency.

address.description: The address of the title agency.

eligible_underwriters.description: The list of title underwriters that the title agency works with.

company_notaries.description: Whether the title agency has company notaries.

logo_image.description:
Supported formats include either a valid, accessible image URL or base64 encoded image file data string.
Most common image formats are supported. Note that successfully imported logo images are resized to 640x640 pixels and
converted to png format.

branding.description: Specifies a list of co-branding related attributes for the partner organization.

email.description: A unique email associated with this organization

logo.description:
Supported formats include either a valid, accessible image URL or base64 encoded image file data string.
Most common image formats are supported. Note that successfully imported logo images are resized to 640x640 pixels and converted to png format.

branding.description: Specifies co-branding related attributes for the partner organization.

activation_time.description: An ISO-8601 formatted DateTime String, optionally including a timezone offset. If timezone offset is not provided, the Proof account’s default timezone settings will be applied. This param sets the time after which the signer is permitted to connect with a notary to complete the transaction. If a signer attempts to connect with a notary before this time is reached, the signer will be only permitted to review documents.

file_number.description: Unique value used to map transactions to a title agency’s records and systems. Including this value helps streamline communications with the title company who is supporting the transaction.

loan_number.description: Unique value used to map transactions to your own records and systems. Providing loan_number as part of a create transaction request ensures it is visible to all users to manage the transaction.

paper_note_consent.description: Allows a lender to create a hybrid transaction and bypass the eNote requirement.

signers.description: An array of signer objects each with at minimum the email field set (limit 10 signers per transaction). The request to notarize the document will be sent to that email address.

recording_jurisdiction_id.description: RECOMMENDED: The id of the recording jurisdiction the property address is within. If this is not provided we will attempt to find the jurisdiction based on the provided street_address.

title_agency_id.description: Required for Lenders. The id of the title agency participating on the transaction. Returned in the /verify_address call. If title_agency_id is empty, contact with role of title_agent is required.

draft.description: When set to true, the transaction will be created in a Draft state. This attribute is typically used when managing multi-document transactions. When the transaction is in draft state, transaction attributes can be modified and additional documents can be added to the bundle, using the documents API. To activate the transaction, the notarization_ready API endpoint must be called.

expiration_time.description: An ISO-8601 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset. If timezone offset is not provided, the account’s default timezone settings will be applied. This param sets time after which the signer is NOT permitted to connect with a notary to complete the transaction. If a signer attempts to complete the transaction after this time is reached, they will receive an error. Expiration time is optional, and no default is set if this parameter is left blank. Any transaction that expires is no longer editable, and a new transaction must be created.

external_id.description: A string representing an ID external to Proof's systems. Use this to associate a transaction to an ID that your systems understand, such as an Order ID or system GUID.

transaction_name.description: Short, human-readable name for the transaction - eg. Miguel's TPS notarization for Leeroy. Defaults to Untitled Transaction.

title_underwriter_id.description: The id of the title underwriter participating on the transaction. Returned in the /verify_address call.

transaction_type.description: Accepted values: refinance, trailing_docs, purchase_seller, purchase_buyer_cash, purchase_buyer_loan, heloc, loan_mod_borrower, other, hybrid_refinance, hybrid_trailing_docs, hybrid_purchase_seller, hybrid_purchase_buyer_cash, hybrid_purchase_buyer_loan, hybrid_other, wet_sign. The refinance and purchase_buyer_loan transaction types require an eNote. Hybrid transactions are notarized off of the Notarize Network platform. Wet sign transactions do not require a `signers` parameter and can only have a document requirement of `esign_supervised`.

message_to_signer.description: Message to signer using GitHub Flavored Markdown. Html, images, links, and code are stripped out. Can be used to provide instructions, thank customers, add additional context to the transaction, or simply to add a personal touch. The message will be added to the email sent to the signer. (30,000 character limit)

message_subject.description: Subject line of email to signer.

pdf_bookmarked.description: Split the document by bookmarks if it is a pdf

require_secondary_id.description:
Certain businesses and transaction types may need signers to provide two forms of photo identification.
This feature enables you to require two forms on a transaction.
If true, the signer cannot proceed to the notary meeting without first providing two forms of photo ID during the Proof identity verification process. If false (default), the signer can proceed to notary meeting after providing one form of photo ID.

suppress_email.description: If sent as true during activation, Proof will not send a notification email to the signer when the transaction is activated. If you use this feature, you are responsible for providing the signer with the `transaction_access_link`. This value is not stored on the transaction and must be provided anytime the transaction is activated.

require_new_signer_verification.description: When set to false, Proof will not require a new signer to verify their email address before moving into the transaction flow.

notary_id.description:
User ID ("us_xxxxxxx") of the assigned Notary.
Eligible notaries must be either a connected BYOT notary, or a BYON notary who is a member of your organization.
Notary assignment is not a strict requirement. When this parameter is specified, the notary will be sent an SMS that the signer is about to enter the waiting room,
 but the notary can take any signer in the queue, and any available notary can take the signer.

notary_meeting_time.description:
An ISO-8061 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset.
Meeting time will be shown to signer in default emails. Proof will not prevent the signer or notary from starting the meeting at any other time.

organization_id.description: The organization id of a child account. If the authenticated account has child accounts, specifying this parameter will create the transaction in the provided child org.

document_url_version.description: Optional param to test v2 document urls.

cc_recipient_emails.description: An array of email addresses to CC on the transaction. They will receive notification when it is sent and completed and be able to view the completed documents.

config_id.description: The ID of the config used for this transaction. This will ensure that the transaction will load as expected in your dashboard if you need to make any edits. Once set this cannot be edited. Available values: ["hybrid_purchase_buyer_loan", "hybrid_refinance", "hybrid_trailing_docs", "identify", "lender_heloc", "lender_loan_mod_borrower", "lender_refinance", "notarization", "other", "purchase_buyer", "purchase_buyer_loan", "purchase_seller", "real_estate_identify", "real_estate_sign", "real_estate_verify", "sign", "title_heloc", "title_loan_mod_borrower", "trailing_docs", "verify"]

bundle_position.description: Position to put document in bundle

activation_time.description: An ISO-8601 formatted DateTime String, optionally including a timezone offset. If timezone offset is not provided, the Proof account’s default timezone settings will be applied. This param sets the time after which the signer is permitted to connect with a notary to complete the transaction. If a signer attempts to connect with a notary before this time is reached, the signer will be only permitted to review documents.

file_number.description: Unique value used to map transactions to a title agency’s records and systems. Including this value helps streamline communications with the title company who is supporting the transaction.

loan_number.description: Unique value used to map transactions to your own records and systems. Providing loan_number as part of a create transaction request ensures it is visible to all users to manage the transaction.

paper_note_consent.description: Allows a lender to create a hybrid transaction and bypass the eNote requirement.

signers.description: An array of signer objects each with at minimum the email field set (limit 10 signers per transaction). The request to notarize the document will be sent to that email address.

recording_jurisdiction_id.description: RECOMMENDED: The id of the recording jurisdiction the property address is within. If this is not provided we will attempt to find the jurisdiction based on the provided street_address.

title_agency_id.description: Required for Lenders. The id of the title agency participating on the transaction. Returned in the /verify_address call. If title_agency_id is empty, contact with role of title_agent is required.

draft.description: When set to true, the transaction will be created in a Draft state. This attribute is typically used when managing multi-document transactions. When the transaction is in draft state, transaction attributes can be modified and additional documents can be added to the bundle, using the documents API. To activate the transaction, the notarization_ready API endpoint must be called.

expiration_time.description: An ISO-8601 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset. If timezone offset is not provided, the account’s default timezone settings will be applied. This param sets time after which the signer is NOT permitted to connect with a notary to complete the transaction. If a signer attempts to complete the transaction after this time is reached, they will receive an error. Expiration time is optional, and no default is set if this parameter is left blank. Any transaction that expires is no longer editable, and a new transaction must be created.

external_id.description: A string representing an ID external to Proof's systems. Use this to associate a transaction to an ID that your systems understand, such as an Order ID or system GUID.

transaction_name.description: Short, human-readable name for the transaction - eg. Miguel's TPS notarization for Leeroy. Defaults to Untitled Transaction.

title_underwriter_id.description: The id of the title underwriter participating on the transaction. Returned in the /verify_address call.

transaction_type.description: Accepted values: refinance, trailing_docs, purchase_seller, purchase_buyer_cash, purchase_buyer_loan, heloc, loan_mod_borrower, other, hybrid_refinance, hybrid_trailing_docs, hybrid_purchase_seller, hybrid_purchase_buyer_cash, hybrid_purchase_buyer_loan, hybrid_other, wet_sign, real_estate_esign, real_estate_proof. The refinance and purchase_buyer_loan transaction types require an eNote. Hybrid transactions are notarized off of the Notarize Network platform. Wet sign transactions do not require a `signers` parameter and can only have a document requirement of `esign_supervised`.

message_to_signer.description: Message to signer using GitHub Flavored Markdown. Html, images, links, and code are stripped out. Can be used to provide instructions, thank customers, add additional context to the transaction, or simply to add a personal touch. The message will be added to the email sent to the signer. (30,000 character limit)

message_subject.description: Subject line of email to signer.

pdf_bookmarked.description: Split the document by bookmarks if it is a pdf

require_secondary_id.description:
Certain businesses and transaction types may need signers to provide two forms of photo identification.
This feature enables you to require two forms on a transaction.
If true, the signer cannot proceed to the notary meeting without first providing two forms of photo ID during the Proof identity verification process. If false (default), the signer can proceed to notary meeting after providing one form of photo ID.

suppress_email.description: If sent as true during activation, Proof will not send a notification email to the signer when the transaction is activated. If you use this feature, you are responsible for providing the signer with the `transaction_access_link`. This value is not stored on the transaction and must be provided anytime the transaction is activated.

notary_id.description:
User ID ("us_xxxxxxx") of the assigned Notary.
Eligible notaries must be either a connected BYOT notary, or a BYON notary who is a member of your organization.
Notary assignment is not a strict requirement. When this parameter is specified, the notary will be sent an SMS that the signer is about to enter the waiting room,
 but the notary can take any signer in the queue, and any available notary can take the signer.

notary_meeting_time.description:
An ISO-8061 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset.
Meeting time will be shown to signer in default emails. Proof will not prevent the signer or notary from starting the meeting at any other time.

organization_id.description: The organization id of a child account. If the authenticated account has child accounts, specifying this parameter will create the transaction in the provided child org.

document_url_version.description: Optional param to test v2 document urls.

documents.description: Documents to be signed or notarized. Accepted as an array of document objects or simple document["resource"] field values.

document.description: Documents to be signed or notarized. Accepted as an array of document objects or simple document["resource"] field values.

require_new_signer_verification.description: When set to false, Proof will not require a new signer to verify their email address before moving into the transaction flow.

skip_closing_ops.description: Send to closing ops or not. Defaults to false.

cc_recipient_emails.description: An array of email addresses to CC on the transaction. They will receive notification when it is sent and completed and be able to view the completed documents.

config_id.description: The ID of the config used for this transaction. This will ensure that the transaction will load as expected in your dashboard if you need to make any edits. Once set this cannot be edited. Available values: ["hybrid_purchase_buyer_loan", "hybrid_refinance", "hybrid_trailing_docs", "identify", "lender_heloc", "lender_loan_mod_borrower", "lender_refinance", "notarization", "other", "purchase_buyer", "purchase_buyer_loan", "purchase_seller", "real_estate_identify", "real_estate_sign", "real_estate_verify", "sign", "title_heloc", "title_loan_mod_borrower", "trailing_docs", "verify"]

role.description: Accepted values: escrow_officer, loan_officer, real_estate_agent, title_agent, closer, other

title.description: Only valid if role is set to "other".

phone_number.description: 10 characters exactly. Dashes and other special characters are not allowed. Please use format "8885551111"

shown_to_signer.description: If false, allows you to set a Proof-only contact.

access_to_transaction.description: Gives the signer contact access to a transaction.

organization_name.description: Use with contact role title_agent to invite a new Title Agent to collaborate on a Proof transaction. If provided for other roles, values will be disregarded.

organization_address.description: Use with contact role title_agent to invite a new Title Agent to collaborate on a Proof transaction. If provided for other roles, values will be disregarded.

email.description: Email is required unless you provide a recipient_group object.

phone_number.description: Signer phone number. A country code can be defined by using the `+` symbol. For example, `+11234567890` will set the country code to `+1` for the number `1234567890`. If no country code is specified the default will be the United States country code of `+1`.

country_code.description: The country code for the phone number, e.g. 1

number.description: The phone number without the country code, e.g. 5551234567

address.description: The most recent known address for the signer.

capacity.description: The signers capacity when signing on behalf of an entity, required when entity is present

entity.description: Entity the signer is signing on behalf of, required when capacity is present

external_id.description: A string representing an ID external to Proof's systems. Use this to associate a signer to an ID that your systems understand, such as a User ID.

link_expired.description: Indicates if the signer's passwordless link has expired.

personally_known_to_notary.description: If the signer is personally known to the notary. This can currently only be used on single signer transactions, or when the notary_id is specified on the signer.

proof_requirement.description: Add enhanced checks to validate a signer's identity to create a Proof transaction. Reach out to your customer success team to enable if not yet available.

recipient_group.description: Use this to send to a group that will be able to claim the transaction. This would be used in place of email and name properties.

sdk_token.description: Equivalent to transaction_access_link; prefer using that instead.

signatory_capacities.description: An array of signatory capacities for the signer when signing on behalf of multiple entities.

signing_requirement.description: Override the document signing requirement for an individual signer.

transaction_access_link.description: A link to the transaction for this signer.

vesting_type.description: Can be either `borrower` or `non_borrower`. Defaults to `borrower`.

notary_id.description: User ID ("us_xxxxxxx") of the assigned Notary or "notarize". Eligible notaries must be a notary who is a member of your organization. Order must be utilized with this field, all notarization signers with the same order must have the same notary_id.

order.description: Order the signers will sign in. Should be positive numbers starting with 1. Setting to the same order as another signer indicates they will sign at the same time.

id_allow_list_by_country.description: A dictionary of id allow lists by country code, e.g. { "US": [ "driver_license", "passport" ], "CA": ["passport"] }, where the key is a 2 digit ISO-3166 country code, and the value is an array of id types. Primary ID lists must only contain government IDs (["driver_license", "passport", "id_card", "passport_card", "work_permit", "permanent_resident_card"]). Secondary ID lists can contain government IDs or supplemental documents (["driver_license", "passport", "id_card", "passport_card", "work_permit", "permanent_resident_card", "social_security_card", "us_visa", "birth_certificate", "property_tax_bill", "voter_registration_card", "organizational_membership_card", "bank_investment_loan_statement", "paycheck_stub", "property_insurance", "utility_bill", "other", "lease", "mortgage", "deed_of_trust", "vehicle_registration", "i_94", "matricula_consular", "nexus_card", "school_id_card"]). By default, validation is only run on the primary ID. Validation can be run on the secondary ID (if it is a government ID) by changing your organization's configuration. To use Proof's default ID options, use null, or do not pass this field.

email.description: Email is required unless you provide a recipient_group object.

phone_number.description: Signer phone number. A country code can be defined by using the `+` symbol. For example, `+11234567890` will set the country code to `+1` for the number `1234567890`. If no country code is specified the default will be the United States country code of `+1`.

country_code.description: The country code for the phone number, e.g. 1

number.description: The phone number without the country code, e.g. 5551234567

address.description: The most recent known address for the signer.

capacity.description: The signers capacity when signing on behalf of an entity, required when entity is present

entity.description: Entity the signer is signing on behalf of, required when capacity is present

external_id.description: A string representing an ID external to Proof's systems. Use this to associate a signer to an ID that your systems understand, such as a User ID.

link_expired.description: Indicates if the signer's passwordless link has expired.

personally_known_to_notary.description: If the signer is personally known to the notary. This can currently only be used on single signer transactions, or when the notary_id is specified on the signer.

proof_requirement.description: Add enhanced checks to validate a signer's identity to create a Proof transaction. Reach out to your customer success team to enable if not yet available.

recipient_group.description: Use this to send to a group that will be able to claim the transaction. This would be used in place of email and name properties.

sdk_token.description: Equivalent to transaction_access_link; prefer using that instead.

signatory_capacities.description: An array of signatory capacities for the signer when signing on behalf of multiple entities.

signing_requirement.description: Override the document signing requirement for an individual signer.

transaction_access_link.description: A link to the transaction for this signer.

vesting_type.description: Can be either `borrower` or `non_borrower`. Defaults to `borrower`.

order.description: Order the signers will sign in. Should be positive numbers starting with 1. Setting to the same order as another signer indicates they will sign at the same time.

notary_id.description: User ID ("us_xxxxxxx") of the assigned Notary or "notarize". Eligible notaries must be a notary who is a member of your organization. Order must be utilized with this field, all notarization signers with the same order must have the same notary_id.

signing_requirement.description: Override the document signing requirement for an individual signer.

state.description: Only two character state abbreviations are accepted. i.e. "CO".

postal.description: Five or ten digit postal code.

country.description: Only two character country codes are accepted. i.e. "US".

full_address.description: The full address written as a single line.

state.description: Only two character state abbreviations are accepted. i.e. "CO".

postal.description: Five or ten digit postal code.

country.description: Only two character country codes are accepted. i.e. "US".

cc_recipient_email.description: Email address of the recipient to be CC'd on the transaction.

video_url.description: Download URL of the video recording of the meeting.

chat_url.description: Download URL of the chat transcript of the meeting.

email.description: Unique email address for the new notary account

us_state_abbr.description: Two-letter abbreviation for the state in which the notary is commissioned.

first_name.description: First name of the notary. Must match their notary commission.

middle_name.description: Middle name of the notary. Must match their notary commission.

last_name.description: Last name of the notary. Must match their notary commission.

enum: Notary is clear to take meetings.
Notary awaiting Proof approval of documentation.

us_state_abbr.description: The two letter abbreviation of the state where this notary is registered.

primary_front.description: URL of the front of the primary photo identification document.

primary_back.description: URL of the back of the primary photo identification document.

primary_type.description: Type of the primary photo identification document.

primary_issuer.description: Issuer of the primary photo identification document.

primary_expiry_date.description: Expiry date of the primary photo identification document.

secondary_front.description: URL of the front of the secondary photo identification document.

requirement.description: Setting indicating whether or not the verify tool will be required to run or pass, or both

knowledge_based_authentication.description: (KBA) Signer answers a series of personal identity questions.

selfie.description: Signer identity is verified through credential analysis and selfie comparison. SMS multi_factor_authentication is required with this for IAL2 compliant identity verification.

type.description: Signer enters authentication code sent to their mobile phone.

shared_inbox_email.description: The email address that is shared by a group.  Whomever has access to this email address can claim the transaction for themself.

allowed_actions.description: List of allowed actions on document.

bundle_position.description: Position to put document in bundle

data.description: base64 encoded document. Present if base64 encoding was specified in request. Currently supported for get_document only.

esign_required.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document requires esigning.

identity_confirmation_required.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document requires identity confirmation.

is_enote.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document is an eNote.

notarization_required.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document requires notarization. If `true`, identity_confirmation_required must be `false`. If `false`, the document may still be e-signed in the notary meeting. At least one document in a transaction must require notarization or identity confirmation.

processing_error.description: Error message if document processing failed.

requirement.description: Completion requirement for the document. Accepted values can be `notarization`, `esign`, `identity_confirmation` **([identity confirmation feature](doc:enterprise-features))**, `readonly`, and `non_essential`. Completion requirement for the document.  If the value is set to `non_essential`, the document will be hidden from signers before the notary meeting and values for  `customer_can_annotate` and `witness_required` will be ignored and set to false. `identity_confirmation` is used to support signing of PS Form 1583 for signers without a US SSN. Note: if `identity_confirmation` is set as the document requirement, ensure that all documents requiring notarization in the transaction also use `identity confirmation` as the requirement to avoid requiring KBA on the transaction. [Read more](doc:document-requirements) about document requirements.

signed_url.description: Signed URL for the final document PDF.

signing_designation_groups.description: This array contains a prescribed list of groups of designations for signers and/or a notary to annotate the document.

signing_designations.description: This array contains a prescribed list of designations for signers and/or a notary to annotate the document.

tracking_id.description: The external tracking ID of the document.

supported.description: Whether erecording is supported at the recording location

eligible.description: Allowed legally and by organizations preferences.

eligible_title_agencies.description: The title agencies that are eligible for the recording location.

eligible_title_underwriters.description: The title underwriters that are eligible for the recording location.

referral_campaign_id.description: The ID of the referral campaign that the transaction is associated with.

referral_code.description: The referral code that the transaction is associated with.

count.description: Number of transactions returned in this call.

total_count.description: Total number of transactions matching this query.

url.description: Valid url to redirect the user to once the meeting is complete

message.description: The message to show the user when the redirect is shown

enote_borrower_object.description: Details needed to register the eNote with MERS eRegistry for post-closing transfers. If there are multiple borrowers on the eNote, please add an object for each borrower.

file.description: Required unless type is EORIGINAL_CONTAINER. An eNote document supplied as a URL or local file encoded as a Base64 string. If type is set, must be an encoded XML file or a URL pointing to an XML. If integration is set, must be a URL pointing to a PDF

type.description: Required unless integration is set. The type of eNote to be added.

integration.description: Required unless type is set. The integration source of the eNote.

document_id.description: Vault ID of an eNote document in eOriginal vault

transaction_id.description: Vault ID of a transaction in an eOriginal vault

transaction_name.description: Name of a transaction in the eOriginal vault.

document_url_version.description: Optional param to test v2 document urls.

signer_identifier.description: For signers, the external_id value set on the signers array when creating a transaction; for witnesses, `witness{n}` i.e. `witness1` or `witness2`; for notaries, `notary`

page_number.description: The page of the designation, starting at 0

type.description: The type of field designation. Accepted values for signers: dob, email, address_line1, address_line2, address_zip5, address_state, address_city, representative_capacity_type. Accepted values for notaries: disclosure, notary_id, seal, commission_expiration_date, commission_state, county, commission_county, principal_id_type, notary_city. Universal types: date_signed, day_signed, month_signed, year_signed, first_name, middle_name, last_name, full_name, signature, initials, checkmark, radio_checkmark, free_text, signature_and_date

optional.description: Non-notary designation that is not part of a group can be set as optional

instruction.description: Instruction specific to this signing designation to be shown to the signer

primary_designation_identifier.description: Unique identification string to denote this designation is the primary designation

conditional_on_primary.description: Identification string that denotes this designation is conditional on the designation with the same value set for primary_designation_identifier

name.description:
The specified name of the group, type string. Use a naming convention of your choice, the only restrictions are that the name must be between 1 and 64 characters
and can consist of alpha-numeric characters, hyphens, parentheses, and underscores. Valid regular expression for you tech nerds is /^[a-zA-Z0-9_-()]{1,64}$/

min_required.description: a positive integer between 0 and N where N is the minimum number of designations in the group that must be fulfilled (checked).

max_required.description:
a positive integer between min_required and N where N is the maximum number of designations in the group that are allowed to be fulfilled.
NOTE: max_required cannot be set to greater than 1 for designation groups used with radio_checkmark type signing designations.

filename.description:
Plain language name given to the document. If this parameter is not provided, Proof will automatically assign a name using the end of the page path of the
resource URL.

resource.description:
The document file resource. Accepted values can be a URL pointing to the PDF file, the full contents of the PDF file in Base64 encoding, the file itself as a
direct upload, or a template permalink string pointing to a preconfigured PDF. The maximum accepted file size is 30MB.

requirement.description:
Completion requirement for the document. Accepted values can be notarization, esign, identity_confirmation (identity confirmation feature), readonly,
and non_essential. Completion requirement for the document. If the value is set to non_essential, the document will be hidden from signers before the notary meeting and values for
customer_can_annotate and witness_required will be ignored and set to false. identity_confirmation is used to support signing of PS Form 1583 for signers without a US SSN.
Note: if identity_confirmation is set as the document requirement, ensure that all  requiring notarization in the transaction also use identity confirmation as the requirement to avoid
requiring KBA on the transaction. Read more about document requirements.

bundle_position.description: Position to put document in bundle

witness_required.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether an additional witness must be present for this document's signing. A witness is a third person, in addition to the
signer and the notary, who must be physically located with the signer.

customer_can_annotate.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether a signer can add their own annotations onto the document. Defaults to false, limiting the signer to only filling
out designated fields setup on the document or as directed by a notary.

pdf_bookmarked.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document is a bookmarked PDF. If passed true the document will be split by bookmark into multiple documents.

tracking_id.description: External tracking identifier such as a UUID string that can be useful for mapping documents back to your own system.

authorization_header.description: Used to fetch doc URLs. Value passed should be in the form of a key-value pair with format "header_name:header_value"

signing_designations.description: This array contains a prescribed list of designations for signers and/or a notary to annotate the document.

signing_designation_groups.description: This array contains a specified list of groups for radio_checkmark and checkmark designations. Grouped designations can be used to logically enforce minimum and/or maximum fulfillment requirements. For example a group of three radio_checkmark designations that require one to be fulfilled (checked) or a group of five checkmark designations that are optional (none or all 5 may be checked).

tracking_id.description: External tracking identifier such as a UUID string that can be useful for mapping documents back to your own system.

requirement.description: Completion requirement for the document. Accepted values can be `notarization`, `esign`, `identity_confirmation` **([identity confirmation feature](doc:enterprise-features))**, `readonly`, and `non_essential`. Completion requirement for the document.  If the value is set to `non_essential`, the document will be hidden from signers before the notary meeting and values for  `customer_can_annotate` and `witness_required` will be ignored and set to false. `identity_confirmation` is used to support signing of PS Form 1583 for signers without a US SSN. Note: if `identity_confirmation` is set as the document requirement, ensure that all documents requiring notarization in the transaction also use `identity confirmation` as the requirement to avoid requiring KBA on the transaction. [Read more](doc:document-requirements) about document requirements.

customer_can_annotate.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether a signer can add their own annotations onto the document. Defaults to false, limiting the signer to only filling
out designated fields setup on the document or as directed by a notary.

notarization_required.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document requires notarization. If `true`, identity_confirmation_required must be `false`. If `false`, the document may still be e-signed in the notary meeting. At least one document in a transaction must require notarization or identity confirmation.

identity_confirmation_required.description: [Enterprise feature.](doc:enterprise-features) Boolean flag indicating whether the document requires identity confirmation. If true, notarization_required must be false. At least one document in a transaction must require notarization or identity confirmation.

witness_required.description: Boolean flag indicating whether an additional witness must be present. This witness is a third person, in addition to the signer and the notary, who must be physically located with the signer. If `true`, this witness must be present. If `false`, no witness is required.

authorization_header.description: Used to fetch doc URLs. Value passed should be in the form of a key-value pair with format "header_name:header_value"

name.description: Plain language name given to the document. If this parameter is not provided, Proof will automatically assign a name using the end of the page path of the resource URL.

document_url_version.description: Optional param to test v2 document urls.

requirement.description:
Completion requirement for the document. Accepted values can be `notarization`, `esign`, `identity_confirmation` **([identity confirmation feature](doc:enterprise-features))**, `readonly`, and `non_essential`.
Completion requirement for the document.  If the value is set to `non_essential`, the document will be hidden from signers before the notary meeting and values for  `customer_can_annotate` and `witness_required` will be ignored and set to false. `identity_confirmation` is used to support signing of PS Form 1583 for signers without a US SSN. Note: if `identity_confirmation` is set as the document requirement, ensure that all documents requiring notarization in the transaction also use `identity confirmation` as the requirement to avoid requiring KBA on the transaction. [Read more](doc:document-requirements) about document requirements.

witness_required.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether an additional witness must be present for this document's signing.
A witness is a third person, in addition to the signer and the notary, who must be physically located with the signer.

customer_can_annotate.description:
Boolean flag indicating whether a signer can add their own annotations onto the document.
Defaults to false, limiting the signer to only filling out designated fields setup on the document or as directed by a notary.

pdf_bookmarked.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the document is a bookmarked PDF. If passed true, the document will be split by bookmark into multiple documents.

tracking_id.description: External tracking identifier such as a UUID string that can be useful for mapping documents back to your own system.

text_tag_syntax.description: Syntax used by text tags within the document.

signing_designations.description: This array contains a prescribed list of designations for signers and/or a notary to annotate the document.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: The organization id of a child account. If the authenticated account has child accounts, specifying this parameter will exclude the authenticated account's transactions and return only the transactions for this child account, as well as all of the child account's children. If this is not provided, we will return all transactions in the authenticated account as well as all transactions in all child accounts.

parameters.name: loan_number
parameters.description: To find transactions associated with the given loan number.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: How many results to return. Max value accepted is 1000.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Return requested # of items starting with the value.

parameters.name: created_date_start
parameters.description: An ISO-8061 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset. Will return all transactions created after this DateTime.

parameters.name: created_date_end
parameters.description: An ISO-8061 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset. Will return all transactions created before this DateTime.

parameters.name: last_updated_date_start
parameters.description: An ISO-8061 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset. Will return all transactions last updated after this DateTime.

parameters.name: last_updated_date_end
parameters.description: An ISO-8061 formatted DateTime String, optionally including the timezone offset. Will return all transactions last updated before this DateTime.

parameters.name: transaction_status
parameters.description: Return transactions based on their current transaction status.
